retrieval_text: Chicory is a culinary medicinal plant profile in the Herbalisti historical corpus. It is represented across 18 works and 22 source-linked chunks. Recorded variants include Chicory. Common plant-part co-mentions include Root, Berry, Flower, Bark, Leaf, Seed. Common preparation co-mentions include Infusion, Juice, Oil, Extract, Syrup, Decoction. Common condition co-mentions include Fever, Diarrhoea or looseness, Headache, Colic, Consumption, Cough. Caution co-mentions include Dose caution, Child-use caution. Representative source works include Dr. Mansperger's book of hygiene : containing instructions for the cure of diseases, together with many valuable and useful prescriptions : added to which is an accurate description of the properties and application of the curative powers of roots and herbs, with instructions for a diagnosis of diseases from the urine and pulse; Cooley's cyclopaedia of practical receipts and collateral information in the arts, manufactures, professions, and trades, including medicine, pharmacy, hygiene, and domestic economy : designed as a comprehensive supplement to the pharmacopoeia and general book of reference for the manufacturer, tradesman, amateur, and heads of families.; A dictionary of domestic medicine and household surgery; A manual of practical hygiene / by Edmund A. Parkes ... Ed. by F. S. B. François de Chaumont.. This is a retrieval summary of historical co-mentions only, not a medical claim.
